banned_phrases.example_critique: Like, 'utilized the atlassian API along with' is so much fluff. Start your sentences/bullets with, what did you build, followed by the tech
the wsib bullet points are very hand-wavey and doesn’t really highlight any real technical proficiencies
for a technical person it's good rn but non technical ppl reading it would just find it too wordy
"demonstrated agility in development" is a nonsense statement
I don't care that you "skillfully implemented change", I'm sure many people can do that
Utilized X scripts is very passive in action description, you’re not committing in your sentences.
that's like just a lot of fluff words after that that don't mean anything

preferred_patterns.example_critique: From Xms to Yms is weak framing unless the numbers are really bold.

preferred_patterns.pattern: Start your sentences/bullets with, what did you build, followed by the tech
preferred_patterns.example_critique: Like, 'utilized the atlassian API along with' is so much fluff. Start your sentences/bullets with, what did you build, followed by the tech

preferred_patterns.pattern: cut down on loosely descriptive sections
preferred_patterns.example_critique: AI is featured prominently in your resume, cut that down
maybe mention it was specifically business dev

preferred_patterns.pattern: use a concepts section to generalize skills
preferred_patterns.example_critique: i've found that adding a concepts section to skills is helpful
Maybe quantify the bullets for your most recent coop.
Explain the features, and explain what the apps do

preferred_patterns.pattern: focus on the narrative of your work
preferred_patterns.example_critique: What were you brought on to solve and why?
I would definitely quantify your co-op experience.
Better to say Wrote X directly.
Without any specific features/improvements I can't tell what the scope of your work was.
I would maybe add some metrics for the CSC discord bot (i.e. how many ppl are in the server/how many ppl are using it).

preferred_patterns.pattern: avoid having only 1 or 2 words on a line
preferred_patterns.example_critique: I would avoid having only 1 or 2 words on a line, either add more or make it more concise.
the first word of your bullet points changes from past tense to present tense a lot.... they should all be past.
it might be helpful to talk about the impact of what this change does, ie. used in X devices or handling X TB of data, or saves Y hours compared to Z.
your P2P network project description is the best one here. It avoids the vague descriptive language to be concise about what you built.
I would order your sections as Skills, Work experience, Projects and Education.

preferred_patterns.pattern: bold keywords or metrics for emphasis
preferred_patterns.example_critique: IMO, i would stick to either bolding your key technologies (Next.js, NEAR, etc.) or your metrics.
I would reword to "via several [concurrent or async/asynchronous] SSH sessions".
you have a tendency to list the same technologies many times.
it is important to try and quanitfy your experiences to show impact.
- Be more specific

preferred_patterns.pattern: add more detail to job points
preferred_patterns.example_critique: add more detail to ur job points add the usage of the tech more specifically
we could consider adding how many people you presented to?
Talk about what some of them were. What you actually did
I would try to add more detail about impact (why/how you built things).
Quantify your bullet points. Right now, you're just listing things you did in your tasks, not showing any impact.
elaborate on what you actually did. quantifying loss of data is very vague, explain how C++ was used, what the business impact was, etc.
try to bold metrics and other important information in the experience section bullet points
having 3 line bullets is bad
remove technician and design team experience since they're less relevant.
Also, how did you develop the teaching materials? This would be a good place to incorporate technologies/tools.
across your projects/experiences, try and come up with quantitative metrics, like "improved x by y percent"
across your projects/experiences, highlight relevant keywords or things you want recruiters to notice by bolding them.
maybe add more about your impact and the results of what you did.
There's nothing wrong with this structure ... but out of 10 bullet points for your experiences, i would say 9 fit this form. it just gets boring.
Just call it software engineer intern
longer sentences tend to be harder to parse. my heuristic is you should only have a small handful of 2-liners in your whole resume. easy first step is replace semicolons with line breaks.

preferred_patterns.pattern: did something using this tech to achieve some metric increase
preferred_patterns.example_critique: I generally structure all my bullet points as 'did something using this tech to achieve some metric increase' to standardize how people read each bullet point
Its also nice to simplify down some sentences

preferred_patterns.pattern: bolding parts to emphasize important contributions
preferred_patterns.example_critique: I personally also bold the parts of each bullet point encapsulate the important technical contributions
you have stripe twice in the last project
If you're looking to work at bigco, like those numbers/changes are semi-meaningless because they are tiny numbers
I think you should give more details in the "Handled the previous bugs and issues related to database", i feel like it's too vague
By continuation, I mean both bullet points belong to the same sentence.
you should expand upon your last two bullet points and include some metrics for impact
Also for the Web Scraper Program project, why did you make it? What was it for?
try to format some of your bullet points to be "impact-oriented".
I would definitely try and quantify your bullet points if possible here to show your impact

preferred_patterns.pattern: replace vague phrases with specific actions
preferred_patterns.example_critique: consider replacing "developed the backend" with more detail
i would simplify the wording of the first project, too many big words
Try to avoid using language that's too technical or domain specific.
just bold your metrics and technologies for a better view
Lowk remove the without marketing.
u gotta apply atleast 400
